scenarios.name: Specific Performance of Sale Agreement

checks.detail: Specific Relief Act / specific performance cited
At least one statutory section cited
Prayer for execution of sale deed
No invented amounts (10L/50L never in facts)

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU (KARNATAKA)

ORIGINAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

ANITA SHARMA, W/o {{HUSBAND_NAME}}, Aged about 52 years, R/o Indiranagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PIN_CODE}}. ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

RAJESH REDDY, S/o {{FATHER_NAME}}, Aged about 60 years, R/o HSR Layout, Bengaluru, Karnataka – {{PIN_CODE}}. ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE OF CONTRACT

PARTIES

1. The Plaintiff is the absolute owner of the funds and is entitled to purchase the immovable property described in the Schedule below. The Plaintiff resides within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

2. The Defendant is the absolute owner of the Schedule Property and is bound by the Agreement to Sell executed with the Plaintiff. The Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court.

FACTS OF THE CASE

3. The subject matter of the suit is the residential flat bearing No. 302, 3rd Floor, Sunshine Apartments, Koramangala, Bengaluru, measuring 1200 sq ft, more fully described in the Schedule of Property annexed hereto.

4. On 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff and Defendant entered into an Agreement to Sell regarding the Schedule Property for a total consideration of Rs. 85,00,000/- (Rupees Eighty Five Lakhs Only).

5. On the date of the agreement, i.e., 10.01.2024, the Plaintiff paid an advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Five Lakhs Only) to the Defendant via RTGS bearing UTR No. HDFC20240110987654.

6. As per the terms of the Agreement to Sell, the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/- was to be paid by the Plaintiff on or before 10.07.2024 against execution and registration of the Sale Deed.

READINESS AND WILLINGNESS

7. The Plaintiff has always been ready and willing to perform her part of the contract. The Plaintiff arranged the balance funds and requested the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice on 20.06.2024 demonstrating her continued readiness and willingness to pay the balance consideration and complete the transaction.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

8. On 05.06.2024, the Defendant sent a letter to the Plaintiff refusing to execute the Sale Deed, claiming that he had received a higher offer for the Schedule Property.

9. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 20.06.2024 calling upon the Defendant to execute the Sale Deed as per the Agreement. The postal receipt for the same is preserved.

10. Despite the legal notice, the Defendant refused to perform his part of the contract and failed to execute the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff.

CAUSE OF ACTION

11. The cause of action first arose on 05.06.2024 when the Defendant sent the letter refusing to execute the Sale Deed.

12. The cause of action continues to subsist as the Defendant has failed to comply with the legal notice dated 20.06.2024 and has not performed his obligations.

LIMITATION

13. The suit is within the period of limitation as prescribed under Article 54 of the Limitation Act, 1963, being within three years from the date fixed for performance or the date of refusal.

JURISDICTION

14. The Schedule Property is situated within the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court. Hence, this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 16 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

COURT FEE

15. The appropriate Court Fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on this plaint as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

INTEREST

16. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest on the advance amount paid. The Plaintiff claims interest @ {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of payment until realization, pre-suit, pendente lite under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, and future interest.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Plaintiff relies on Section 10 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963, which mandates specific performance of contracts relating to immovable property. Section 16 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 requires the Plaintiff to plead readiness and willingness, which is pleaded in Paragraph 7.

18. Section 21 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 allows for compensation in lieu of or in addition to specific performance. Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963 allows for possession to be awarded. Section 10 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 establishes that agreements by free consent are contracts. Section 53A of the Transfer of Property Act, 1882 is invoked regarding part performance. Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 confers jurisdiction on civil courts.

PRAYER

19. The Plaintiff prays for a judgment and decree:
(a) Directing the Defendant to specifically perform the Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024 and execute the Sale Deed in favour of the Plaintiff upon payment of the balance consideration of Rs. 60,00,000/-;
(b) Directing the Defendant to deliver possession of the Schedule Property to the Plaintiff under Section 22 of the Specific Relief Act, 1963;
(c) Awarding interest @ {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the advance amount of Rs. 25,00,000/- from the date of payment until realization;
(d) Awarding costs of the suit;
(e) Granting pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908;
(f) Granting such other relief as this Hon'ble Court deems fit in the interests of justice.

VERIFICATION

20. I, Anita Sharma, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 19 are true to my knowledge and based on legal advice which I believe to be true. Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DAY}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

ANNEXURE-A: Original Agreement to Sell dated 10.01.2024.
ANNEXURE-B: RTGS Receipt with UTR No. HDFC20240110987654.
ANNEXURE-C: Legal Notice
Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION) AT BENGALURU, KARNATAKA

COMMERCIAL SUIT NO. _____ OF 2024

BETWEEN:

TECHSOFT SOLUTIONS PVT LTD, A COMPANY REGISTERED UNDER THE COMPANIES ACT, HAVING ITS REGISTERED OFFICE AT MG ROAD, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PIN}}, ACTING THROUGH ITS DIRECTOR PRIYA MENON, AGED ABOUT 40 YEARS, R/O {{ADDRESS}} ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

CLOUDBRIDGE SYSTEMS LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP HAVING ITS OFFICE AT WHITEFIELD, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA – {{PIN}}, ACTING THROUGH ITS DESIGNATED PARTNER VIKRAM SINGH, AGED ABOUT 48 YEARS, R/O {{ADDRESS}} ... DEFENDANT

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT AND OTHER RELIEFS

1. The Plaintiff is a company registered under the Companies Act, having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru, Karnataka, acting through its Director Priya Menon, aged about 40 years. The Company is engaged in the business of software solutions and technology services.

2. The Defendant is a Limited Liability Partnership having its office at Whitefield, Bengaluru, Karnataka, acting through its Designated Partner Vikram Singh, aged about 48 years. The Defendant is engaged in the business of software development and systems integration.

3. This Court has jurisdiction to try this suit under Section 9 and Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, as the Defendant resides and carries on business within Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within the territorial limits of this Court.

4. This suit is filed before the Commercial Court as the dispute is a commercial dispute under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and the specified value of the suit is Rs. 32,00,000/-, which is above the threshold limit of {{AMOUNT}} prescribed under the said Act.

5. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff entered into a Software Development Agreement with the Defendant for the development of an ERP system for a total contract value of Rs. 45,00,000/-. The terms of the agreement obligated the Defendant to develop and deliver specific software modules within stipulated timelines.

6. Pursuant to the Agreement, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as the first milestone payment to the Defendant via NEFT on 01.02.2024. The transaction is evidenced by UTR: ICIC20240201456789.

7. Under the terms of the Agreement, the Defendant was contractually obligated to deliver the first module of the ERP system by 01.05.2024. Time was the essence of the contract regarding the delivery milestones.

8. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024. Despite reminders, the Defendant did not provide the contracted deliverables within the stipulated time.

9. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without delivering any module and without returning the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/- paid by the Plaintiff.

PARTICULARS OF BREACH AND LOSS

10. The Defendant breached the contract by failing to deliver the first module by 01.05.2024, violating the delivery obligations under the Software Development Agreement.

11. The Defendant further breached the contract by abandoning the project on 15.06.2024 and failing to refund the advance payment of Rs. 20,00,000/-, thereby causing financial loss to the Plaintiff.

12. The Plaintiff has suffered a total loss of Rs. 32,00,000/-, comprising Rs. 20,00,000/- as refund of advance payment and Rs. 12,00,000/- as consequential damages due to delay in operations caused by non-delivery, as assessed by the auditor.

13. The cause of action arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project and failed to deliver the module or refund the advance. The cause of action is a single event arising on the date of breach.

14. The suit is within limitation as per Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which prescribes a period of three years from the date when the contract is broken. The suit is filed within the prescribed period.

15. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice to the Defendant dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to refund the amount and compensate for the losses. The Defendant failed to comply with the notice.

16. The Plaintiff has issued a legal notice and has complied with the provisions of Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 regarding pre-institution mediation as applicable to this suit.

17. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest at the rate of 18% per annum on the claimed amount under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, from the date of breach until realisation, including pre-suit, pendente lite, and future interest.

18. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid on this plaint as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, applicable to the Commercial Court.

19. PRAYER: The Plaintiff prays for judgment and decree against the Defendant for: (a) Recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- being the refund of advance payment;
(b) Recovery of Rs. 12,00,000/- being consequential damages for breach of contract;
(c) Interest at 18% per annum on Rs. 32,00,000/- from 15.06.2024 until realisation;
(d) Cost of the suit;
(e) Any other relief this Court deems fit; and (f) Such other relief as the Plaintiff is entitled to under law.

20. LIST OF DOCUMENTS: The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents listed in Annexure-A: (1) Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024; (2) NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789); (3) Email correspondence showing abandonment; (4) Loss assessment report by auditor; (5) Legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

21. STATEMENT OF TRUTH: I, Priya Menon, Director of TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd, the Plaintiff herein, do hereby declare and verify on oath that the contents of Paragraphs 1 to 20 of this Plaint are true and correct to my knowledge and belief, and nothing material has been concealed therefrom. This Statement of Truth is made pursuant to Order XI Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 as applicable to Commercial Courts. Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DAY}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

PLACE: BENGALURU
DATE: {{DATE}}

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}

ADVOCATE
ENROLLMENT NO.: {{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
ADDRESS: {{ADDRESS}}
MOBILE: {{MOBILE}}

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF
